labels.label: OCR-ing takes place here, please make sure you have ocr-service/ocr-service-text-only Docker container running, and the ports 8090 (for ocr-ing) / 8091 (text extraction only) are free. Note: if both services are running on the same host as NiFi use port 8090 for both urls: http://ocr-service:8090/api/process or http://ocr-service-text-only:8090/api/process

We split the json because we want 1 record per flowfile, so that we can later update the attributes of the flowfile withe the record's UUID.

IMPORTANT: note the path expression : $.*

Notice that the ElasticsearchJson processor supports only insertion by attribute name, as opposed to the ElasticsearchRecord processor that takes the desired identifier directly from the avro flowfile.
Simple ingestion from PgSQL with OCR to ElasticSearch
Check the EvaluateJsonPath, we add a new property called "id" with the value "$.id" , takes the value from the json.
